title: Flaky Vietnamese Cornmeal Pancakes
description: Fermented, flaky egg‑free pancakes made with white cornmeal, rice flour, and a touch of coconut milk—perfect for a hangover cure or a light breakfast.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together cornmeal, rice flour, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Add the warm water, cold water, and coconut milk, stirring until a thick batter forms.
Cover the bowl with a damp cloth and let the batter ferment at room temperature for 30–45 minutes until it becomes bubbly and slightly sour.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and lightly brush with oil.
Pour a small ladleful of batter onto the skillet, spreading it into a thin circle; the batter should be spread quickly to keep it thin.
Cook 2–3 minutes until the edges lift and the bottom is golden.
Fold the pancake in half, then fold the halves in a criss‑cross pattern to create a flaky, layered texture.
Flip the folded pancake and cook another 2 minutes until the other side is golden.
Transfer to a plate and serve warm; optionally garnish with chopped cilantro or scallions.
